title: populus × canadensis var. marilandica
intro: Populus × canadensis var. marilandica, also known as the Swamp Cottonwood or Carolina Poplar, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its broad, triangular leaves and cotton-like seeds. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Populus × canadensis var. marilandica include reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the bark or leaves in hot water or mixing them with a carrier oil.
